question: Does a Matryoshka-over-bits cascade (w-word packed sketch scan -> top-C1 -> 256-bit Hamming on survivors -> top-C2 -> f32 rerank) beat the flat 2-tier funnel at recall parity? Tier A uses two-pass histogram selection (no per-doc dists buffer), tile=8, same rotation x2 + residual codes.

sift_1m_control.note: cascade also dominated on SIFT1M/128-d: c1=50000 -> 0.8318 @ 1844 vs funnel 0.8334 @ 1572 (only ~parity at 5% corpus C1)

verdict: NEGATIVE at recall parity: every cascade point is Pareto-dominated by the flat funnel (which reaches 0.9737 @ 847 and 0.922 @ 902). The 64-bit sketch is statistically too weak at N=10M (Hamming concentrates at 32 +/- 4; true neighbors are not reliably in the top 0.25-4% by sketch distance). Wider sketches fix recall but pay back the byte saving: two-pass histogram selection reads the sketch twice, so a w-word sketch costs 2w/4 of the full scan's stream - at w=2 that is byte-identical to the flat 256-bit scan and adds C1 random gathers on top.
when_it_could_win: Only if tier A were single-pass (needs a selection that keeps ids without knowing the threshold: bigger-than-C1 loose heap, or sampled threshold estimation) AND the sketch were 64-bit AND target recall <= ~0.93. A niche not worth engine complexity; parked.
